hazeSeasCodes.title: Haze Seas Codes
hazeSeasCodes.intro: Redeem Haze Seas codes from the in-game menu through the Treasure Chest or Gift code window for free cash, race spins, gems, EXP boosts, and stat resets. New codes usually tie to updates, trailers, community milestones, and group rewards.

items.name: GROUPONLY
items.description: Active — join the Haze Studios Roblox group

unlockMilestones: Launch Haze Seas from the official Roblox page
Select the Treasure Chest or Gift code icon
Paste the code exactly as written
Press Redeem and claim the reward

hazeSeasOfficialLinks.title: Haze Seas Official Links
hazeSeasOfficialLinks.intro: Haze Seas uses multiple official channels: Trello for structured game data, Discord for live announcements and fruit stock, Roblox for the game and group, YouTube for trailers and showcases, and X for quick update posts.

cards.name: Haze Seas Official Trello
cards.description: The main structured reference for Game Info, codes, controls, Chest System, Mastery System, Bounty System, Sea Events, Devil Fruits, Swords, Fighting Styles, Races, NPCs, Bosses, and locations.

cards.name: Haze Studios Discord
cards.description: The live community hub for game announcements, hotfixes, code drops, fruit stock tracking, questions, and bug reports.

cards.name: Haze Seas Roblox Game Page
cards.description: The official Roblox experience page for HAZE PIECE and the Haze Seas rework, including device support, game-pass notes, and fruit spawn information.

cards.name: Haze Studios Roblox Group
cards.description: The official Haze Studios community page on Roblox. Group membership also unlocks group-only rewards such as the GROUPONLY code.

cards.name: Haze Studios YouTube
cards.description: The official video channel for Haze Seas trailers, release videos, fruit awakening cutscenes, and gameplay showcases.

cards.name: Haze Seas X
cards.description: The official X account for quick Haze Seas announcements, trailer posts, showcase clips, and code-related update posts.

hazeSeasBeginnerGuide.title: Haze Seas Beginner Guide
hazeSeasBeginnerGuide.intro: Haze Seas is the Haze Studios Roblox action RPG rework built around islands, enemies, bosses, fruits, swords, fighting styles, races, mastery, bounty, and sea events. Use this starter flow to progress smoothly from your first island to late-game routes.

steps.title: Launch the Official Roblox Experience
steps.description: Start from the official HAZE PIECE Roblox page, which is tied to the Haze Seas rework by Haze Studios. Favorite or follow the experience before joining a server.

steps.title: Pick Your Device Setup
steps.description: The game page lists Xbox and mobile support, so you can start on PC, mobile, or console. Use the device you control most comfortably and check the Trello controls card before fighting bosses.

steps.title: Read Game Info Before Grinding
steps.description: The Trello Game Info column covers controls, codes, Chest System, Mastery System, and Bounty System. Check Game Info first, then branch into Devil Fruits, Swords, Fighting Styles, Races, NPCs, and Bosses.

steps.title: Claim Codes Early
steps.description: Codes reward cash, race spins, gems, EXP boosts, and stat resets, which matter most before a long grinding session. Open Menu, select the Treasure Chest or Gift code icon, paste a code, and redeem before leveling.

steps.title: Choose a Simple First Build
steps.description: Keep enough Defense to survive while choosing whether your main damage path will come from Fruit, Sword, Fighting Style, or Combat. Pick one main damage route and use Trello to compare related skills.

steps.title: Track Devil Fruits
steps.description: Fruits spawn every 60 minutes and despawn after 20 minutes. VIP servers can hold fruit spawns with a 5-fruit limit. Use the Discord fruit-stock channel and Trello fruit cards before swapping fruits.

steps.title: Use Discord for Live Game Status
steps.description: The Discord server separates announcements, codes, fruit stock, questions, and bug reports into channels. Check game-announcement for hotfixes, codes for rewards, and fruit-stock for tracking.

steps.title: Watch Official Showcases
steps.description: Haze Studios posts trailers, release videos, fruit awakening scenes, and showcase clips on YouTube. Use official videos to preview combat style, fruit animations, and update direction before committing to a build.

quickTips: Treat the official Trello and Discord as part of your core toolkit.
Pick one main damage route instead of spreading every stat evenly.
Check fruit stock on Discord before spending cash, gems, or rerolls.
Keep enough Defense to survive while your damage route comes online.

hazeSeasDevilFruits.title: Haze Seas Devil Fruits
hazeSeasDevilFruits.intro: Devil Fruits are one of the main build paths in Haze Seas. Fruit choices shape grinding speed, PvP pressure, mobility, and boss farming. Use this hard data to plan spawns, stock, and awakenings.

solutions.name: Fruit Spawn Timer
solutions.description: A fruit spawns on the map every 60 minutes. Check the map or stock channel around the hourly timer instead of searching randomly.

solutions.name: Fruit Despawn Timer
solutions.description: A spawned fruit despawns after 20 minutes. Move quickly after a fruit appears, especially on public servers where other players are also hunting.

solutions.name: VIP Server Fruit Limit
solutions.description: Fruits can spawn in VIP servers with a listed limit of 5. Use VIP servers for cleaner fruit hunting routes and fewer interruptions.

solutions.name: Fruit Stock Tracking
solutions.description: The official Discord includes a fruit-stock channel for live Devil Fruit stock tracking. Check stock before spending cash, gems, or rerolls.

solutions.name: Fruit Data Cards
solutions.description: The official Trello separates Devil Fruits into their own reference area alongside Swords, Fighting Styles, Races, NPCs, and Bosses. Open the fruit card before replacing your current fruit.

solutions.name: Known Fruit Roster
solutions.description: Clear, Chop, Spin, Spike, Bomb, Barrier, Paw, Smoke, Sand, String, Mammoth, Buddha, Snow, Tremor, Gas, Gravity, Flame, Shadow, Light, Operation, Ice, Electricity, Magma, Love, Gum, Darkness, Magnet, Phoenix, Soul, Leopard, Venom, Dragon, and Dough.

solutions.name: Awakenings
solutions.description: Haze Studios has published a Haze Seas fruit awakening cutscene video, making awakenings a front-page topic for fruit players.

solutions.name: Grinding Use
solutions.description: For leveling, players want fruit moves that clear groups, travel between islands, or damage bosses safely. Mark fruits by farming role after testing moves and cooldowns.

solutions.name: PvP Use
solutions.description: For PvP, fruit value depends on how well the move kit creates openings, extends combos, avoids punishment, or forces opponents to block. Separate PvP notes from grinding notes.

managementTips: Check the Discord fruit-stock channel before hunting on the map.
Use VIP servers for cleaner fruit routes and fewer interruptions.
Compare moves, mastery requirements, and awakenings on Trello before swapping.
Separate grinding fruits from PvP fruits when planning your build.

hazeSeasTierList.title: Haze Seas Tier List
hazeSeasTierList.intro: This Haze Seas tier list ranks the most searched build choices across Devil Fruits, weapons, races, and fighting styles so you can compare each system side by side. Use it as a practical reference when deciding what to keep, reroll, farm, or upgrade next across grinding, bosses, PvP, and sea events.

achievements.title: Dough
achievements.description: S Tier · Mythical — High-value fruit with strong combo potential for PvP, boss damage, and late-game builds.

achievements.title: Dragon
achievements.description: S Tier · Mythical Zoan — Powerful transformation pressure for bossing, transformation builds, and high-end PvP.

achievements.title: Phoenix
achievements.description: S Tier · Legendary — Strong recovery, mobility, and safer progression for survival and flexible PvE.

achievements.title: Leopard
achievements.description: A Tier · Legendary Zoan — Aggressive close-range pressure and mobile PvP combat.

achievements.title: Venom
achievements.description: A Tier · Legendary — Damage over time, boss pressure, and area control with poison zones.

achievements.title: Magma
achievements.description: A Tier · Rare Logia — Reliable damage and area attacks for grinding and boss farming.

achievements.title: Light
achievements.description: A Tier · Rare Logia — Mobility, fast progression, and safe ranged farming.

achievements.title: Gum
achievements.description: B Tier · Legendary — Flexible combo play and ability-focused builds.

achievements.title: Magnet
achievements.description: B Tier · Legendary — Spacing, ranged pressure, and ability-based damage.

achievements.title: Ice
achievements.description: B Tier · Rare Logia — Freeze-style crowd control for safe grinding and PvP setups.

achievements.title: Flame
achievements.description: B Tier · Rare Logia — Accessible early-to-mid game damage for farming.

achievements.title: Gravity
achievements.description: B Tier · Rare — Larger ability impact for area pressure and ability damage.

achievements.title: Smoke
achievements.description: C Tier · Uncommon Logia — Early elemental option before stronger fruits unlock.

achievements.title: Sand
achievements.description: C Tier · Uncommon Logia — Serviceable early farming fruit, easy to replace later.

achievements.title: Bomb
achievements.description: C Tier · Common — Basic starter damage before rare or legendary routes open.

achievements.title: Chop
achievements.description: C Tier · Common — Early utility fruit with limited long-term value.

achievements.title: Yoru / Dark Blade
achievements.description: S Tier · Mythical Sword — Premium PvP sword builds via shop, gifting, or trading.

achievements.title: Enma
achievements.description: S Tier · Legendary Sword — Late-game sword damage from the Enma Boss drop route.

achievements.title: 3 Sword Style
achievements.description: S Tier · Legendary Sword — Combo-heavy multi-sword pressure from the 3SS Boss route.

achievements.title: Sea Beast Hammer V2
achievements.description: S Tier · Legendary Weapon — Sea-event farming from the White Sea Beast drop route.

achievements.title: Golden Staff
achievements.description: A Tier · Legendary Sword — Electricity-linked builds from the Thunder God route on Sky Islands.

achievements.title: Raiu
achievements.description: A Tier · Legendary Sword — Fast sword pressure from the Shiryu Scroll route at Skull Island.

achievements.title: Bisento V2
achievements.description: A Tier · Rare Sword — Upgraded Bisento from the Tremor Girl super boss route.

achievements.title: Shusui
achievements.description: A Tier · Legendary Sword — Thriller Boat progression from the Ryummy drop route.

achievements.title: Sea Beast Hammer
achievements.description: B Tier · Legendary Weapon — Heavy damage and sea-event farming from the Sea Beast route.

achievements.title: Operation Blade
achievements.description: B Tier · Legendary Sword — Ability-themed sword builds from the Magma Minion route.

achievements.title: 2 Sword Style V2
achievements.description: B Tier · Rare — Mid-to-late sword combos from the Dual Swordsman route.

achievements.title: Mace V2
achievements.description: B Tier · Legendary Weapon — Upgraded heavy weapon pressure from the Mace V2 Boss route.

achievements.title: Katana
achievements.description: C Tier · Common Sword — Starter sword play from the sword shop before boss drops.

achievements.title: Shark Blade
achievements.description: C Tier · Rare Sword — Early boss-drop sword from the Shark Boss at Shark Park.

achievements.title: Soul Cane
achievements.description: C Tier · Rare Sword — Transitional mid-game utility from the Mr Skeleton route.

achievements.title: 2 Sword Style
achievements.description: C Tier · Rare — Early sword combos from Mr White before farming the V2.

achievements.title: D. Clan
achievements.description: S Tier · Mythical — Endgame PvP, mobility, and survivability with regen, speed, SkyWalk, Observation Haki, stamina, health, and Conquerors Spirit.

achievements.title: Dragonoid
achievements.description: S Tier · Legendary — Observation Haki builds and durable combat with regen, health, and jump power.

achievements.title: Lunarian
achievements.description: S Tier · Legendary — Mobility and sustain with SkyWalk, regen, jump power, and stamina.

achievements.title: Demon
achievements.description: A Tier · Rare — Fast aggression and PvP movement with a major walk-speed bonus.

achievements.title: Celestial Dragon
achievements.description: A Tier · Rare — Quest money and balanced progression with health and stamina support.

achievements.title: Mink
achievements.description: B Tier · Uncommon — Speed, jumping, and early movement with walk speed, jump power, and stamina.

achievements.title: Fishman
achievements.description: B Tier · Uncommon — Swimming and durable progression with health and swim-speed bonuses.

achievements.title: Cyborg
achievements.description: B Tier · Uncommon — Tankier early progression with health and health regeneration.

achievements.title: Skypeian
achievements.description: B Tier · Uncommon — Jumping and stamina with a simple movement-focused kit.

achievements.title: Human
achievements.description: C Tier · Common — Default starter race with no major build-defining bonus.

achievements.title: 3 Eyed Tribe
achievements.description: C Tier · Common — Minor stamina bonus for early support.

achievements.title: Elf
achievements.description: C Tier · Common — Minor health regeneration bonus for early support.

achievements.title: Dragonic / Dragon Claw
achievements.description: S Tier · Advanced Style — Late-game melee pressure for aggressive close-range combat.

achievements.title: Cyborg
achievements.description: S Tier · Advanced Style — Burst damage and ability-based melee for advanced builds.

achievements.title: Fish-Man Karate
achievements.description: A Tier · Style — Balanced PvE and PvP melee pressure with a practical all-round kit.

achievements.title: Electro
achievements.description: A Tier · Style — Speed-focused melee and stun pressure for fast attackers.

achievements.title: Black Leg
achievements.description: B Tier · Style — Practical first melee upgrade after basic Combat.

achievements.title: Combat
achievements.description: C Tier · Starter Style — Default melee kit before unlocking stronger styles.

hazeSeasRaces.title: Haze Seas Races
hazeSeasRaces.intro: Races in Haze Seas affect movement, health, stamina, regeneration, Haki access, and long-term build direction. Use early race spins carefully when deciding whether to keep a rare, legendary, or mythical race for grinding, PvP, or sea progression.

regions.name: D. Clan
regions.description: 0.1% roll · X-shaped scar. Grants health regen, walk speed, SkyWalk, Observation Haki, stamina, health, and Conquerors Spirit. Best for endgame PvP and mobility. Keep.

regions.name: Dragonoid
regions.description: 1% roll · Dragon wings and tail. Health regen, Observation Haki, health, and jump power. Best for durable builds and late-game progression. Keep.

regions.name: Lunarian
regions.description: 1% roll · Black wings and fire orb. SkyWalk, health regen, jump power, and stamina. Best for mobility, sustain, and vertical movement. Keep.

regions.name: Demon
regions.description: 8.9% roll · Demon horns and black tail. Walk speed, health, and stamina. Best for aggressive PvP movement and fast questing. Keep unless chasing legendary or mythical.

regions.name: Celestial Dragon
regions.description: 8.9% roll · Glass helmet. Quest money, health, and stamina. Best for money farming and balanced progression. Keep for farming.

regions.name: Mink
regions.description: 35% roll · Rabbit ears. Walk speed, jump power, and stamina. Best for movement and mobility-focused grinding. Keep early, reroll later.

regions.name: Fishman
regions.description: 35% roll · Shark fin. Health and swim speed. Best for sea movement and island-to-island travel. Keep early if travel speed matters.

regions.name: Cyborg
regions.description: 35% roll · Science tank. Health and health regeneration. Best for safer early combat and durability. Keep early, reroll later.

regions.name: Skypeian
regions.description: 35% roll · Angel wings. Stamina and jump power. Best for jumping, stamina, and early movement. Keep early, reroll later.

regions.name: Human
regions.description: 55% roll · No visible trait. Default starter race with no build-defining bonus. Reroll when spins are available.

regions.name: 3 Eyed Tribe
regions.description: 55% roll · Third eye. Minor stamina bonus for early support. Reroll when spins are available.

regions.name: Elf
regions.description: 55% roll · Elf ears. Minor health regeneration bonus for early support. Reroll when spins are available.

hazeSeasWeapons.title: Haze Seas Weapons and Fighting Styles
hazeSeasWeapons.intro: Weapons and fighting styles define how a Haze Seas build plays when fruit abilities are on cooldown or when you want a sword-focused route. Use this comparison to decide what to buy early, farm from bosses, and target for late-game PvP or sea events.

settings.name: Yoru / Dark Blade
settings.description: Shop, gifting, or trading route (1,200 Robux). Premium PvP sword builds for serious sword investment.

settings.name: Enma
settings.description: Defeat Enma Boss (10% drop). Major late-game sword damage target.

settings.name: 3 Sword Style
settings.description: Defeat 3SS Boss (5% drop). Combo-focused multi-sword pressure.

settings.name: Sea Beast Hammer V2
settings.description: Defeat White Sea Beast (3.5% drop). Sea-event weapon builds.

settings.name: Golden Staff
settings.description: Defeat Thunder God on Sky Islands (1% drop). Electricity-linked sword builds.

settings.name: Raiu
settings.description: Shiryu Scroll route at Skull Island. Fast sword pressure for late progression.

settings.name: Shusui
settings.description: Defeat Ryummy at Thriller Boat (boss drop). Late-game sword progression.

settings.name: Fishman Trident
settings.description: Fishman Island weapon route. Water-themed sword builds while progressing the island.

settings.name: Bisento
settings.description: Purchase from Bella NPC (500,000 Money). Reliable purchased sword before harder drops.

settings.name: Bisento V2
settings.description: Defeat Tremor Girl (20% drop). Upgraded Bisento for super boss farming.

settings.name: Operation Blade
settings.description: Magma Minion route (250,000 Money). Ability-themed sword builds.

settings.name: Sea Beast Hammer
settings.description: Defeat Sea Beast at Rocky Pillars (50% drop). Heavy damage and sea-event farming.

settings.name: Mace
settings.description: Defeat Mace Boss on Winter Island (100% drop). Guaranteed heavy melee route.

settings.name: Mace V2
settings.description: Defeat Mace V2 Boss (1% drop). Upgraded heavy weapon pressure.

settings.name: 2 Sword Style
settings.description: Purchase from Mr White (100,000 Money). Early sword combos.

settings.name: 2 Sword Style V2
settings.description: Defeat Dual Swordsman at Logue City (10% drop). Mid-game sword combos.

settings.name: Shark Blade
settings.description: Defeat Shark Boss at Shark Park (5% drop). Early boss-drop sword.

settings.name: Katana
settings.description: Sword shop purchase. Starter sword before rare and legendary routes open.

settings.name: Combat
settings.description: Default fighting style. First levels and basic melee.

settings.name: Black Leg
settings.description: Trainer at Sea Restaurant (Money purchase). Practical first melee upgrade.

settings.name: Electro
settings.description: Trainer at Udon Prison (Money purchase). Fast melee and stun pressure.

settings.name: Fish-Man Karate
settings.description: Trainer on Fishman progression route. Balanced PvE and PvP melee.

settings.name: Cyborg
settings.description: Advanced trainer route. Burst-focused ability-based melee builds.

settings.name: Dragonic / Dragon Claw
settings.description: Advanced trainer route. Late-game aggressive melee pressure.

hazeSeasBosses.title: Haze Seas Bosses Islands and Sea Events
hazeSeasBosses.intro: Haze Seas progression is built around island routes, boss farming, item drops, super bosses, and sea events. Use this map and drop guide to decide what to farm next as you push from the First Sea into late-game routes.

entries.title: Shark Park — Shark Boss (Lv 120)
entries.description: Drops Shark Blade. Farm for an early rare sword before pushing deeper into First Sea progression.

entries.title: Desert Ruins — Bomb Boss (Lv 200)
entries.description: Boss progression route used mainly for leveling toward higher-level island content.

entries.title: Sea Restaurant — Krieg Boss (Lv 300)
entries.description: Drops Gold Pauldrons. Early accessory farming and Black Leg progression.

entries.title: Logue Town — Tashii (Lv 400)
entries.description: Drops Glasses. Continue farming before moving into mid-game boss routes.

entries.title: Tall Woods — King Gorilla (Lv 550)
entries.description: Drops Monkey Crown. Checkpoint for players building accessory collections.

entries.title: Marine Base Town — Marine Captain (Lv 650)
entries.description: Drops Iron Jaw and Lava Ore. Farm for boss progression and upgrade materials.

entries.title: Three Islands — Minotaur (Lv 750)
entries.description: Drops Oversized Helmet. Mid-game stop before Marine HQ and Sky Islands.

entries.title: Marine HQ — Ice Admiral (Lv 900)
entries.description: Drops Sleeping Mask and TremorBeard Key. Prepares for special boss routes.

entries.title: Sky Islands — Thunder God (Lv 1100)
entries.description: Drops Golden Staff and Thunder Drums. Farm for the Golden Staff and electricity-themed routes.

entries.title: Revolutionary Base — Revolutionary Boss (Lv 1250)
entries.description: Boss progression route before pushing into prison and late First Sea zones.

entries.title: Impel Jail — Warden (Lv 1400)
entries.description: Drops Warden Hat and Match. Key stop before Hot Island and Fishman Island.

entries.title: Hot Island — Vergo (Lv 1500)
entries.description: Drops Black Shades. Accessory farming and late First Sea progression.

entries.title: Cold Island Lab — Harpy (Lv 1550)
entries.description: Drops Ice Ore. Tied to Sea Beast Core and Blacksmith crafting progression.

entries.title: Fishman Island — Neptune (Lv 1700)
entries.description: Drops Neptune Crown and Fork. Farm for Fishman-related equipment routes.

entries.title: Skull Island — Shiryu (Lv 1850)
entries.description: Drops Scroll for Raiu progression. Key stop for late First Sea weapon routes.

entries.title: Bubble Island — G4 Boss (Lv 2000)
entries.description: Drops Gear 4 Book. Important for Gear 4-related progression.

entries.title: Thriller Boat — Ryummy (Lv 2150)
entries.description: Drops Shusui and Blue Scarf. Major late First Sea sword-farming target.

entries.title: Logue City — Dual Swordsman (Lv 500)
entries.description: Drops 2 Sword Style V2, Green Bandana, and Book. Farm after planning 2 Sword Style progression.

entries.title: Super Boss — Tremor Girl
entries.description: Drops Bisento V2. Upgrade route for Bisento-style sword combat.

entries.title: Winter Island — Mace Boss (Lv 700)
entries.description: Drops Mace. Guaranteed heavy weapon route before rarer upgrades.

entries.title: Late Boss — Enma Boss
entries.description: Drops Enma. Farm when ready to chase a stronger legendary sword.

entries.title: Late Boss — 3SS Boss
entries.description: Drops 3 Sword Style. Key target for multi-sword combo builds.

entries.title: Late Boss — Mace V2 Boss
entries.description: Drops Mace V2. Upgraded heavy weapon path for Mace route players.

entries.title: Rocky Pillars — Sea Beast (Lv 700)
entries.description: Drops Sea Beast Hammer, Sea Beast Core, Money, Gems, and Bounty. Heavy weapon and material farming.

entries.title: Sea Route — White Sea Beast
entries.description: Drops Sea Beast Hammer V2. Farm for the upgraded Sea Beast Hammer path.

entries.title: Udon Prison — Mammoth Route
entries.description: Second Sea progression with an Electro trainer route for advanced melee.

entries.title: Winter Island — Snow Boss Route
entries.description: Late progression island route through higher-level Second Sea content.

entries.title: Snake Amazon — Late Island Route
entries.description: Late progression stop before stronger fruit and boss routes.

entries.title: Venom Island — Venom Boss
entries.description: Venom route progression. Farm for late-game ability and boss content.

entries.title: Late Boss — Dough User
entries.description: Connected to high-demand mythical Dough fruit progression.

entries.title: Late Boss — Dragon Boss
entries.description: Connected to high-demand mythical Zoan Dragon progression.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Haze Seas

questions.question: What is Haze Seas?
questions.answer: Haze Seas is the Haze Studios Roblox pirate action RPG rework of HAZE PIECE, built around islands, Devil Fruits, swords, fighting styles, races, bosses, bounty, and sea events.

questions.question: How do I redeem Haze Seas codes?
questions.answer: Launch Haze Seas, open the Menu button, select the Treasure Chest or Gift code icon, paste a code exactly as written, and press Redeem to claim the reward on the same account.

questions.question: How often do Devil Fruits spawn in Haze Seas?
questions.answer: In Haze Seas, a Devil Fruit spawns every 60 minutes and despawns after 20 minutes. VIP servers can hold fruit spawns with a 5-fruit limit, and live stock is tracked in the Discord fruit-stock channel.

questions.question: Where can I find official Haze Seas links?
questions.answer: The official Haze Seas channels are the Trello board for structured data, the Haze Studios Discord for live updates, the Roblox game and group pages, the Haze Studios YouTube for trailers, and the Haze Seas X account for quick posts.

questions.question: How do races and race spins work in Haze Seas?
questions.answer: Haze Seas races affect movement, health, stamina, regeneration, and Haki access. Use race spins to reroll, and keep rare, legendary, or mythical rolls that match your grinding, PvP, or sea progression goals.

questions.question: Is Haze Seas free to play on Roblox?
questions.answer: Yes. Haze Seas is a Roblox experience playable on PC, mobile, and console through the official HAZE PIECE Roblox game page.

cta.title: Ready to Set Sail in Haze Seas?
cta.description: Join the Haze Seas community on Discord for codes, fruits, races, guides, updates, and discussions!

codes.title: Haze Seas Codes
codes.description: Latest working Haze Seas codes for free race spins, gems, stat resets, and trait rerolls. Redeem them in-game for faster pirate progression.
codes.metaTitle: Haze Seas Codes - Free Gems & Race Spins
codes.metaDescription: Get the latest active Haze Seas codes for free gems, race spins, stat resets, and trait rerolls with step-by-step redemption instructions.
codes.whatIsDescription: Haze Seas codes are free redeem strings released by Haze Studios that grant bonus rewards such as gems, race spins, stat resets, and trait rerolls. New codes drop alongside major updates, milestones, and seasonal events.

whySteps.step1Description: Codes grant gems, stat resets, and spins that speed up leveling, fruit rolling, and race changes without grinding.
whySteps.step2Description: New codes ship with each content patch, so checking regularly keeps you ahead of fresh fruits, weapons, and bosses.
whySteps.step3Description: Trait and race rerolls from codes let you test builds without spending premium currency.

guide.title: Haze Seas Guide
guide.description: Complete Haze Seas guides for beginners, leveling, bosses, drops, haki, and the Second Sea. Master progression, combat, and exploration.
guide.metaTitle: Haze Seas Guide - Beginner to Pro Walkthrough
guide.metaDescription: Master Haze Seas with full guides on leveling, haki, bosses, drops, Second Sea, and progression from beginner to pro pirate.
guide.whatIs: What Is the Haze Seas Guide?
guide.whatIsDescription: The Haze Seas guide hub covers every system you need to progress, from first-spawn beginner tips and fastest leveling routes to boss strategies, drop tables, haki unlocks, and the Second Sea transition.
guide.why: Why Read the Haze Seas Guide?

whySteps.step1Description: Optimized grinding routes and stat builds help you reach max level without wasted effort.
whySteps.step2Description: Boss breakdowns with level requirements, attack patterns, and drop tables take the guesswork out of fights.
whySteps.step3Description: Step-by-step prerequisites and progression milestones guide you into the Second Sea content.

fruits.title: Haze Seas Fruits
fruits.description: Every Haze Seas devil fruit covered: abilities, rarities, awakening, and rework details. Find the best fruit for PvP, grinding, and bossing.
fruits.metaTitle: Haze Seas Fruits - Abilities, Rarities & Awakenings
fruits.metaDescription: Browse all Haze Seas devil fruits with abilities, rarities, awakening paths, and rework updates to pick the best fruit for PvP and grinding.
fruits.whatIsDescription: Devil fruits are the signature power system in Haze Seas, granting unique elemental and combat abilities. Each fruit has its own rarity, moveset, awakening path, and role in PvP, grinding, or boss fights.

whySteps.step1Description: Compare abilities and rarities to choose a fruit that fits your playstyle and progression goals.
whySteps.step2Description: Awakening paths and reworks reshape a fruit's moveset, so knowing the route saves reroll time.
whySteps.step3Description: Understanding fruit matchups and combos improves performance in PvP and boss encounters.

weapons.title: Haze Seas Weapons
weapons.description: Full Haze Seas swords and weapons list with locations, stats, and rework details. Find the best blade for your build and playstyle.
weapons.metaTitle: Haze Seas Weapons & Swords - Locations & Stats
weapons.metaDescription: Discover every Haze Seas sword and weapon with locations, stats, rarities, and rework updates to build the strongest loadout.
weapons.whatIsDescription: Weapons and swords are core combat gear in Haze Seas, each with unique locations, stats, attack patterns, and rarities. Reworked weapons add refreshed movesets and balance updates that shift the meta.

whySteps.step1Description: Exact locations and acquisition methods mean you never miss a strong sword on your journey.
whySteps.step2Description: Stats and rarities help you compare options and pair weapons with the right fruit and fighting style.
whySteps.step3Description: Weapon reworks rebalance damage and movesets, so tracking updates keeps your build competitive.

styles.title: Haze Seas Fighting Styles
styles.description: Haze Seas fighting styles like Black Leg and Electro explained: moves, unlock requirements, and best use cases for PvP and grinding.
styles.metaTitle: Haze Seas Fighting Styles - Black Leg, Electro & More
styles.metaDescription: Learn every Haze Seas fighting style including Black Leg and Electro with moves, unlock requirements, and best uses for PvP and grinding.
styles.whatIs: What Are Haze Seas Fighting Styles?
styles.whatIsDescription: Fighting styles are melee combat disciplines in Haze Seas that define your close-range moveset. Styles like Black Leg and Electro each offer distinct combos, mobility, and unlock paths for PvP and grinding.
styles.why: Why Learn Haze Seas Fighting Styles?

whySteps.step1Description: Each style unlocks unique combos and mobility options that complement fruit and weapon builds.
whySteps.step2Description: Clear requirements tell you exactly when and where to pick up each fighting style.
whySteps.step3Description: Different styles excel in PvP versus grinding, so choosing the right one maximizes efficiency.

release.whatIs: What Is Haze Seas Release Info?
release.whatIsDescription: The release section tracks Haze Seas launch dates, major updates, rerelease milestones, and the Haze Piece revamp that redefined the game. It covers what shipped and when, from launch dates to content patches.
release.why: Why Follow Haze Seas Release Info?

whySteps.step1Description: Update notes tell you which fruits, weapons, bosses, and balance changes arrived in each patch.
whySteps.step2Description: The Haze Piece revamp timeline explains how the game evolved into the current Haze Seas experience.
whySteps.step3Description: Knowing release and rerelease dates helps you time your grind around fresh content drops.
